message:
fix(ci): require at least one successful build before publishing release

The release-publish job previously only checked !cancelled(), which could
publish an empty release if all build jobs failed. Now it also requires
at least one of release-build or release-build-lite to succeed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -749,7 +749,9 @@ jobs:
 
   release-publish:
     name: Publish GitHub Release
-    if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v') && !cancelled()
+    if: >-
+      startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v') && !cancelled()
+      && (needs.release-build.result == 'success' || needs.release-build-lite.result == 'success')
     needs: [release-build, release-build-lite]
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
     timeout-minutes: 20
